summary: A high‑energy audience tape that captures a blistering Shakedown, soaring Saint of Circumstance and an epic Morning Dew with crystal‑clear crowd vibes.
review: Fans rave about the show’s explosive first‑set opener (Shakedown), Phil’s powerhouse bass on Saint of Circumstance, Jerry’s monster Deal solo and a transcendent Morning Dew that closes the night. The George Bethke audience recording is generally clear and vivid, though a few reviewers note occasional bass‑peak distortion.

ai_rating.rationale: All ten reviews are five‑star and overwhelmingly praise the performance; recording is praised as clear with only minor technical hiccups, yielding a combined high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall lively and detailed capture of band and crowd; occasional bass‑peak distortion on Shakedown, Saint, Truckin' and Dew noted by a few reviewers.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong opening with Shakedown, seamless transitions through a fiery first set (Deal closing) and an electrifying second set featuring Help‑Slip‑Frank and a soaring Dew finale; crowd energy is described as palpable throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers highlight Jerry's explosive solo on Deal and his tone on Saint of Circumstance, calling it "off the hook" and "floored".
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work is praised as “all over” the show, especially his blasting start on Shakedown.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brief mention of a "Brent number" (keyboard spotlight) but no detailed commentary on Keith/Brent/Vince performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum segment after the Bobby jam is noted as "mighty fine"; overall rhythm section praised.
